commands.review_summary:
The PR prevents cron-tool from auto-stamping the caller sessionKey onto isolated cron adds and changes missing cron.remove IDs to return INVALID_REQUEST, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +51. Total +60 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main's cron tool stamps the active sessionKey after normalization, and cron.remove returns success even when removed is false. I did not run a live WebUI/Gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair lane: the branch is a plausible narrow fix, but the external contributor needs to add real behavior proof and maintainers need to accept the stricter remove behavior.

target.title: fix(cron): honor isolated sessionTarget and fail missing cron.remove id
target.body:
## Summary
- prevent cron tool auto-injection of caller session key when a cron job explicitly targets sessionTarget: isolated
- make cron.remove return an invalid-request error when the target job id is not found, instead of ok: true, removed: false

## Why
sessionKey auto-stamping for isolated cron jobs can bind isolated runs to caller/dashboard session lanes and cause collisions. Also, successful-looking removals for missing ids hide real failures.

Fixes #86202

## Validation
- CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts
- CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/cron.validation.test.ts
